description: FD vs best WA rows for paper fig03 and Table tab:fd_accuracy. VERIFIED 2026-05-27 with AC protocol FD recomputation on AutoDL GPU.
fd_computation_protocol: DistributionShiftProbe (online WavLM features, mean pooling, max_samples=500, seed=42, AC suite 70/15/15 hash split)

rows.fd_note: C-BESD vs IEMOCAP feature distributions. IEMOCAP indomain WA reflects training ON IEMOCAP (not zero-shot).

rows.label_zh: 风格+自发性偏移（C-BESD→FAU，FAU 域内）
rows.fd_note: C-BESD vs FAU Aibo feature distributions. FAU indomain WA reflects training ON FAU (not zero-shot). Same FD as zero-shot row but higher WA shows in-domain training mitigates distribution gap.

rows.label_zh: 风格+自发性偏移（零样本 C-BESD→FAU）
rows.fd_note: Same distribution gap as FAU indomain row (FD=8.50), but zero-shot WA=19.56% vs indomain WA=66.36% illustrates the value of in-domain training for overcoming distribution shift. WA updated 2026-05-27 to test-only split (N=3389).

c_besd_vs_cremad.description: C-BESD (child acted, Malay) vs CREMA-D (adult acted, English). Combined age+language+recording shift. Much larger FD than C-BESD vs IEMOCAP (7.20) due to added language/recording divergence.
c_besd_vs_cremad.note: Not used in fd_accuracy_table fig03 (uses IEMOCAP for age shift, FAU for style shift). Included for reference.
